prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(register-integration_target prod_runtime_instance)
(register-integration_target staging_runtime_instance)
(register-integration_target integrator_alpha)
(register-integration_target integrator_beta)
(select-library_for_target prod_runtime_instance lib_candidate_a)
(select-library_for_target staging_runtime_instance lib_candidate_b)
(select-library_for_target integrator_alpha lib_candidate_c)
(attach-api_binding prod_runtime_instance api_binding_adapter_v1)
(confirm-api_binding prod_runtime_instance api_binding_adapter_v1)
(detach-api_binding prod_runtime_instance api_binding_adapter_v1)
(attach-api_binding staging_runtime_instance api_binding_adapter_v1)
(confirm-api_binding staging_runtime_instance api_binding_adapter_v1)
(detach-api_binding staging_runtime_instance api_binding_adapter_v1)
(attach-api_binding integrator_alpha api_binding_adapter_v1)
(confirm-api_binding integrator_alpha api_binding_adapter_v1)
(detach-api_binding integrator_alpha api_binding_adapter_v1)
(attach-api_binding prod_runtime_instance api_binding_adapter_v1)
(reserve-runtime_environment prod_runtime_instance prod_runtime_env_descriptor api_binding_adapter_v1)
(apply-configuration_template prod_runtime_instance config_template_default)
(stage-runtime_component prod_runtime_instance prod_runtime_env_descriptor config_template_default)
(remove-configuration_template prod_runtime_instance config_template_default)
(detach-api_binding prod_runtime_instance api_binding_adapter_v1)
(attach-api_binding staging_runtime_instance api_binding_adapter_v1)
(reserve-test_environment staging_runtime_instance staging_env_descriptor api_binding_adapter_v1)
(apply-configuration_template staging_runtime_instance config_template_default)
(stage-test_component staging_runtime_instance staging_env_descriptor config_template_default)
(remove-configuration_template staging_runtime_instance config_template_default)
(detach-api_binding staging_runtime_instance api_binding_adapter_v1)
(attach-api_binding prod_runtime_instance api_binding_adapter_v1)
(assemble-connector_with_both_envs prod_runtime_instance staging_runtime_instance prod_runtime_env_descriptor staging_env_descriptor connector_build_base)
(mark-connector_verified connector_build_base prod_runtime_instance api_binding_adapter_v1)
(detach-api_binding prod_runtime_instance api_binding_adapter_v1)
(mark-runtime_component_ready_for_deployment prod_runtime_instance connector_build_base)
(mark-test_component_ready_for_deployment staging_runtime_instance connector_build_base)
(attach-api_binding prod_runtime_instance api_binding_adapter_v1)
(bind-credential_to_target prod_runtime_instance credential_integration_key api_binding_adapter_v1)
(activate-integration_on_runtime_component prod_runtime_instance lib_candidate_a credential_integration_key)
(select-library_for_target integrator_beta lib_candidate_a)
(attach-api_binding integrator_beta api_binding_adapter_v1)
(confirm-api_binding integrator_beta api_binding_adapter_v1)
(detach-api_binding integrator_beta api_binding_adapter_v1)
(apply-config_file_to_connector integrator_alpha connector_config_v1 connector_build_base)
(attach-api_binding integrator_alpha api_binding_adapter_v1)
(prepare-integrator_for_validation integrator_alpha connector_config_v1 connector_build_base api_binding_adapter_v1)
(detach-api_binding integrator_alpha api_binding_adapter_v1)
(attach-policy_to_integrator integrator_alpha security_policy_s1)
(attach-observability_hook integrator_alpha observability_hook_prometheus)
(apply-configuration_template integrator_alpha config_template_default)
(attach-observability_and_validate_integrator integrator_alpha observability_hook_prometheus config_template_default connector_config_v1 connector_build_base)
(attach-compatibility_report integrator_alpha compat_report_initial)
(validate-integrator_compatibility integrator_alpha compat_report_initial connector_config_v1 connector_build_base)
(detach-observability_hook integrator_alpha observability_hook_prometheus)
(remove-configuration_template integrator_alpha config_template_default)
(detach-compatibility_report integrator_alpha compat_report_initial)
(auto-approve_release integrator_alpha)
(apply-config_file_to_connector integrator_beta connector_config_v2 connector_build_base)
(attach-api_binding integrator_beta api_binding_adapter_v1)
(prepare-integrator_for_validation integrator_beta connector_config_v2 connector_build_base api_binding_adapter_v1)
(detach-api_binding integrator_beta api_binding_adapter_v1)
(attach-policy_to_integrator integrator_beta license_policy_s2)
(attach-observability_hook integrator_beta observability_hook_prometheus)
(apply-configuration_template integrator_beta config_template_default)
(attach-observability_and_validate_integrator integrator_beta observability_hook_prometheus config_template_default connector_config_v2 connector_build_base)
(attach-compatibility_report integrator_beta compat_report_initial)
(validate-integrator_compatibility integrator_beta compat_report_initial connector_config_v2 connector_build_base)
(detach-observability_hook integrator_beta observability_hook_prometheus)
(remove-configuration_template integrator_beta config_template_default)
(detach-compatibility_report integrator_beta compat_report_initial)
(auto-approve_release integrator_beta)
(attach-api_binding integrator_alpha api_binding_adapter_v1)
(bind-credential_to_target integrator_alpha credential_integration_key api_binding_adapter_v1)
(propagate-credential_to_integrator integrator_alpha lib_candidate_c credential_integration_key)
(attach-api_binding integrator_beta api_binding_adapter_v1)
(bind-credential_to_target integrator_beta credential_integration_key api_binding_adapter_v1)
(propagate-credential_to_integrator integrator_beta lib_candidate_a credential_integration_key)
(attach-api_binding staging_runtime_instance api_binding_adapter_v1)
(bind-credential_to_target staging_runtime_instance credential_integration_key api_binding_adapter_v1)
(activate-integration_on_test_component staging_runtime_instance lib_candidate_b credential_integration_key)
message.reasoning:
The problem is a complex PDDL planning domain for third-party library integration. The goal is to deploy integration on prod_runtime_instance, staging_runtime_instance, integrator_alpha, and integrator_beta.


## Examining Deployment Scope
- The task involves deploying a third-party library integration across multiple environments.
- Target instances include prod_runtime_instance, staging_runtime_instance, integrator_alpha, and integrator_beta.
- The problem is framed as a complex PDDL planning domain, suggesting structured planning is needed.

## Examining Deployment Scope
- Available resources include libraries (lib_candidate_a, b, c), api_binding_adapter_v1, config_template_default, and example_helper_snippet.
- Environments are linked: prod_runtime_env_descriptor to prod_runtime_instance, staging_env_descriptor to staging_runtime_instance.
- Config files (v1, v2) and connector_build_base are accessible for deployment.

## Identifying Key Assignments
- integrator_alpha and integrator_beta are assigned to prod_runtime_instance, staging_runtime_instance, and connector_build_base.
- integrator_alpha uses connector_config_v1, while integrator_beta uses connector_config_v2.
- Additional resources include security_policy_s1, license_policy_s2, approval tokens, credentials, observability hooks, and performance targets.

## Analyzing Integration Targets
- All instances (prod_runtime_instance, staging_runtime_instance, integrator_alpha, integrator_beta) are integration_targets, categorized under component_role_category or integrator_role_category.
- prod_runtime_instance is a runtime_component, staging_runtime_instance is a test_component, and both integrators fall under integrator_role_category.

## Outlining Deployment Process
- Deployment involves registering each integration_target, selecting a library, attaching api_binding, and confirming it for configuration.
- Additional steps include applying configuration templates, with specific actions for components like staging, and binding credentials before activation.

## Outlining Deployment Process  
- Attaching and confirming api_binding_adapter_v1 is a step for both prod_runtime_instance and staging_runtime_instance, but can be detached if needed.  
- For staging_runtime_instance, the process starts with registration, then selecting lib_candidate_b, followed by attaching and confirming the api_binding.  

## Managing Library Selection  
- Selecting a library, like lib_candidate_a for integrator_alpha, consumes it, making it unavailable for others until activation.  
- This means full deployment of one target must complete before selecting the same library for another, due to shared resource constraints.  

## Handling Integrator Deployment  
- Deploying integrators like integrator_alpha requires credentials_bound, which depends on release_approved_for_target and specific integrator flows.  
- These flows involve prevalidation, observability, compatibility checks, and approval, often needing a packaged and verified connector.  

## Configuring Connector and Components  
- Packaging the connector requires staging components, reserving environments, and having connector_build_available, with actions like assemble_connector_*.  
- Configuration uses config_template_default, which can be applied to one target at a time, requiring removal and reuse via remove_configuration_template if needed.

## Staging Components  
- Configuring prod_runtime_instance involves attaching api_binding, reserving environments, and applying config_template_default to stage it.  
- After staging, remove_configuration_template can restore its availability for use with staging_runtime_instance, ensuring both components can be staged sequentially.  

## Managing Environment Resources  
- Staging both prod and test components requires reserving environments and applying the configuration template, but only one target can use it at a time.  
- The process allows detaching and reattaching api_bindings as needed to manage shared resources like environments and templates effectively.  

## Assembling Connectors  
- Assembling a connector for both environments requires both components to be staged and ready, with environments reserved and a connector build available.  
- Verification of the connector needs the api_binding reattached to prod_runtime_instance, as detaching it earlier affects the verification step.

## Preparing Integrator Validation  
- Preparing an integrator for validation, like integrator_alpha, requires it to be configured with an applied config file, such as connector_config_v1, and an associated connector.  
- The process involves ensuring the config file is available, applied, and linked, with prerequisites like a packaged and verified connector.  

## Managing Config Files  
- Applying a config file to a connector, such as connector_build_base, consumes the file, making it unavailable for others until removed, similar to library selection.  
- This ensures sequential use, with effects setting the file as applied and associated, but initial availability is true for versions like v1 and v2.  

## Setting Validation Readiness  
- After applying the config, preparing for validation sets prevalidation_ready, requiring no connector_flag_runtime_ready, which aligns with using assemble_with_both_envs without flags.  
- This step doesn’t require policies, focusing on config and observability, ensuring the integrator is ready for further validation steps.  

## Attaching Observability  
- Attaching observability, like observability_hook_prometheus, requires prevalidation_ready and an applied config template, ensuring integrator observability is configured.  
- This step involves validating the integrator, building on prior config and connector associations, to prepare for compatibility checks.

## Managing Environment Reservations  
- Reserving environments for runtime and test components, like in assemble_with_both_envs, keeps them attached until deployment, ensuring availability for staging.  
- Managing the single api_binding requires careful attachment to either prod or staging based on current needs, balancing shared resource use.  

## Selecting Libraries  
- Selecting libraries for integrators, like lib_a for prod and lib_b for staging, consumes them, leaving lib_c for alpha initially, with no lib left for beta until deployment.  
- Deploying a component, such as prod, restores its library (e.g., lib_a), enabling selection for subsequent targets like beta after earlier deployments.  

## Preparing Integrator Deployment  
- Binding credentials to integrators, like bind_credential for alpha, requires an approved integrator and an attached api_binding, consuming available credentials.  
- Propagating credentials to integrators, such as propagate_credential_to_integrator, ensures deployment, using available libraries and credentials restored after component deployment.

## Selecting Libraries  
- Deploying a component, like prod, restores its library (e.g., lib_a), making it available for subsequent selections, such as for beta.  
- After deploying prod, lib_a becomes available, allowing selection for beta, which was previously blocked due to all libraries being consumed.  

## Preparing Integrator Deployment  
- Configuring an integrator, like beta, requires library selection first, which can only happen after deploying another component to free up a library.  
- For beta, after selecting lib_a post-prod deployment, attach binding and confirm configuration, ensuring readiness for further steps.  

## Managing Environment Reservations  
- Managing the api_binding involves attaching it to different targets, like prod for deployment and beta for preparation, requiring careful coordination.  
- After deploying prod, the binding can be detached and reattached to beta, ensuring availability for subsequent integrator steps.  

## Handling Performance and Policies  
- Beta uses license_policy_s2, with performance_target_latency initially set but not utilized in the current path.  
- Compatibility reports and observability hooks, like observability_hook_prometheus, need detachment before switching targets, ensuring no conflicts.

## Preparing Integrator Deployment  
- Staging and prod environments are prepared by reserving test environments, applying default configuration templates, and staging components.  
- After staging, api_binding is detached from both prod and staging, making them ready for further assembly and verification.

## Assembling and Verifying Connectors  
- Connectors are assembled using both prod and staging environments, with api_binding attached to prod for verification.  
- Once verified, the connector is marked as packaged and ready, with api_binding detached afterward.

## Deploying Components  
- Prod is deployed by attaching api_binding, binding credentials, and activating integration with lib_candidate_a, freeing up lib_a.  
- After deployment, credentials and lib_a become available, enabling library selection for subsequent targets like beta.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
